appraisal_events.evidence: « Elle n'avait dû partir que pour obtenir de moi de meilleures conditions, plus de liberté, de luxe... celui qui l'eût emporté de nous deux, c'eût été moi, si j'eusse eu la force d'attendre... même l'emportant à la fin... je serais le vaincu. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator presents Albertine's departure as a maneuver that gives her the advantage and admits that his inability to 'durer' yields to her the position of strength, so that even a later return would leave him the loser in his own calculation.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, Albertine gains emotional leverage over the narrator thanks to her absence, perceived as a means of obtaining concessions and setting the terms of the relationship.
